title: SACRAMENTO, CA —
prompt_preview:
[1 chunks] Task: Extract all distinct claims about AI or emerging technology in this press release. Do NOT assume a fixed number.
Output JSON only matching the provided schema.
Return concise, standalone claim_texts that could be tested against scientific literature. Exclude purely procedural statem

claims.claim_text: Exposure to PFAS has been linked to serious health harms including cancer, cardiovascular diseases, reproductive harm, and developmental issues in infants and children.
Recent tests suggest that nearly 12 million Californians are exposed to tap water with PFAS contamination.
The Biden Administration EPA established the first-ever national drinking water standards to protect Americans from PFAS exposure.
Sustained exposure to PFAS can cause cancer and other illnesses, and that PFAS exposure is especially dangerous during pregnancy and early childhood.
Activated carbon, reverse osmosis, and ion exchange systems can help to protect against PFAS exposure.
California currently lags behind at least 11 other states in regulating PFAS.
Setting enforceable state limits on PFAS would help to prevent thousands of deaths and serious illnesses.
The Bipartisan Infrastructure Law includes $21 billion to help upgrade drinking water systems to incorporate technologies that address PFAS.
